error_signature: workflow re-executing with same ID after 65 seconds
title: Workflow re-execution due to long Wait node duration
symptom: Workflows using a Wait node for over 64 seconds re-execute with the same execution ID, causing interruptions. The previous execution stops shortly after the re-execution begins.
applies_when: Using Wait node in queue mode
Wait duration is 65 seconds or more
not_this_if: Wait duration is less than 60 seconds
root_cause: In queue mode, n8n has a limitation where workflows with long blocking nodes, such as Wait nodes exceeding 60 seconds, can trigger re-execution. This is due to the way execution locks are managed, leading to potential overlaps in workflow executions.
fix_steps: Replace Wait node with a Schedule trigger or Execute Workflow node
If using loops, add a short Wait (1 second) between iterations
Monitor execution with N8N_LOG_LEVEL=debug to identify issues
agent_instruction: Replace long Wait nodes with alternative scheduling methods and implement short pauses in loops.
